description: Create the Daytona HTTP routes and integrate the service into the Express server. Register API endpoints for sandbox management, wire the Daytona service into the server startup/shutdown lifecycle, and create the WebSocket proxy endpoint that relays traffic between the frontend and OpenCode inside Daytona sandboxes. This connects the Daytona module to the existing server infrastructure.
steps: Create packages/web/server/lib/daytona/routes.js - Register Daytona sandbox management routes following the registerXxxRoutes(app, dependencies) pattern used by other modules. Endpoints: POST /api/daytona/sandbox (create a new sandbox for a chat session, body: { sessionId }), DELETE /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId (destroy sandbox, supports the exit command), GET /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId/status (get sandbox status and health), GET /api/daytona/sandboxes (list all active sandboxes), POST /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId/activity (heartbeat endpoint to reset inactivity timer).

Create packages/web/server/lib/daytona/proxy.js - OpenCode proxy for Daytona sandboxes. Similar to packages/web/server/lib/opencode/proxy.js but routes requests to the OpenCode instance inside the sandbox instead of the local managed process. Registers: GET /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId/event (SSE forwarder from sandbox OpenCode), POST /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId/message (message forwarder to sandbox OpenCode), and generic /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId/opencode/* forwarding for all other OpenCode API calls.

Wire the Daytona service into packages/web/server/index.js: Import createDaytonaService, initialize it during server startup (after settings are loaded so env vars are available), register routes with the Express app, add graceful shutdown of all sandboxes in the shutdown handler (alongside existing OpenCode process shutdown), and conditionally enable the service only when DAYTONA_API_KEY is set.

Add WebSocket upgrade handling for the Daytona sandbox terminal/event connections. Follow the pattern used by the existing terminal WebSocket in packages/web/server/lib/terminal/runtime.js. The WS endpoint at /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId/ws should proxy WebSocket traffic to the OpenCode instance running in the sandbox.
Update packages/web/server/lib/opencode/shutdown-runtime.js to also call daytonaService.dispose() during graceful shutdown if the service is active.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/daytona/sandbox creates a new sandbox and returns { sandboxId, status, openCodeUrl }
DELETE /api/daytona/sandbox/:sessionId destroys the sandbox and returns success
SSE and message proxy endpoints correctly relay to sandbox OpenCode
Server starts without errors when DAYTONA_API_KEY is not set (service disabled gracefully)
Server integrates the Daytona service when DAYTONA_API_KEY is set
Graceful shutdown destroys all active sandboxes
verification: Run 'bun run type-check:web' and confirm no type errors
Run 'bun run lint:web' and confirm no lint errors
Run 'bun run --cwd packages/web test' and confirm all tests pass
findings: All files created and verified. routes.js registers 5 endpoints (POST create, DELETE destroy, GET status, GET list, POST heartbeat) at /api/daytona/*. proxy.js implements SSE forwarding, message relay, generic OpenCode API proxy via http-proxy-middleware, and WebSocket upgrade handler (attachDaytonaWsProxy). Server index.js integration: imports added at line 94-96, service initialized after relay service registration (line ~1400), conditionally enabled only when DAYTONA_API_KEY is set, shutdown disposal added to async stop handler. The stop function was changed from sync to async to support await on daytonaService.dispose(). All 639 existing tests pass, lint clean, type-check clean.
